response_body_utf8: {"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2026,7,13]],"date-time":"2026-07-13T01:10:20Z","timestamp":1783905020760,"version":"3.55.0"},"posted":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"group-title":"SSRN","reference-count":0,"publisher":"Elsevier BV","content-domain":{"domain":[],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":[],"abstract":"<jats:p>Customs duties are a primary public-revenue source in many developing economies, yet administrations examine only a small fraction of declarations, so protecting revenue means allocating scarce audit capacity to those most likely to conceal evaded duty. The supporting analytics are trained and evaluated only on inspected cases, and because inspection is selective, the labels are missing not at random: reported performance is biased, the policy inherits historical selection, and never-inspected declarations stay unobserved. We recast audit targeting as estimation and decision under selective labels, exploiting post-clearance audit, which under our assumptions reaches the full population on observable features. Using it as a full-support, ignorably-assigned arm, we define a recoverable-duty estimand identified by a cross-fitted doubly-robust estimator, certify it with marginal-sensitivity and assumption-free bounds, target robustly against confounding, and attach selection-valid conformal intervals with calibrated abstention. In a synthetic data-generating process calibrated to realistic operating points, the design estimator recovers recoverable duty to within about two percent where clearance-arm estimators err by about half, the inspected-only evaluation is biased against the selection-robust policy by sixteen points, that policy captures about forty percent more than a clearance-trained ranking, and selection-valid conformal coverage on the never-inspected band is attainable only from the post-clearance arm. These figures are properties of the simulation and require validation on real audit data. The method yields a deployable prescription for public-sector audit planning: a minimum post-clearance audit rate on uninspected bands.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.2139\/ssrn.7102071","type":"posted-content","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,7,13]],"date-time":"2026-07-13T00:35:32Z","timestamp":1783902932000},"source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["Protecting Customs Revenue with Scarce Audit Capacity: Selection-Robust Targeting of Post-Clearance Undervaluation under Label Bias"],"prefix":"10.2139","author":[{"ORCID":"https:\/\/orcid.org\/0009-0007-8071-6971","authenticated-orcid":true,"given":"Sushanta","family":"Paul","sequence":"first","affiliation":[],"role":[{"vocabulary":"crossref","role":"author"}]}],"member":"78","container-title":[],"original-title":[],"deposited":{"date-parts":[[2026,7,13]],"date-time":"2026-07-13T00:35:32Z","timestamp":1783902932000},"score":1,"resource":{"primary":{"URL":"https:\/\/www.ssrn.com\/abstract=7102071"}},"subtitle":[],"short-title":[],"issued":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"references-count":0,"URL":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.2139\/ssrn.7102071","relation":{},"subject":[],"published":{"date-parts":[[2026]]},"subtype":"preprint"}}
